summary: Large gm_1601 decomp/matching PR split out from jj/the-big-gm-pr. It replaced many gm_1601.c stubs with C implementations, updated prototypes in gm_1601.h, and refined gm/types.h layouts needed by the new code. The decomp.dev bot reported +1624 matched bytes overall, with two newly fully matched functions, gm_80166CCC and fn_801656A8, plus broad partial-match improvements across 18 gm_1601 items.

key_files.role: Main decompilation work: 1205 added lines across 33 hunks, replacing many stub comments with gm_1601 implementations and matching helpers.
Prototype corrections from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS and narrower types to concrete return and argument types for matched gm_1601 functions.
Struct layout refinements exposing fields used by the gm_1601 implementations, including lbl_8046B6A0_24C_t and MatchPlayerData offsets.

what_changed: Implemented/matched many gm_1601.c stubbed functions, with the PR body explicitly naming fn_80165AC0, fn_80165FA4, gm_8016AC44, fn_80169A84, fn_80169C54, fn_80169000, fn_80167638, fn_80161C90, fn_80161154, fn_8016A4C8, gm_80166CCC, fn_80164B48, and earlier gm_1601 work.
Added helper includes needed by the implementations: placeholder.h, if/ifnametag.h, and pl/plbonus.h.
Introduced wrapper/noinline-style helper functions around gm_801601C4, gm_80160244, gm_80160980, fn_801609E0, and gm_80164840 to influence call/inlining behavior for matching.
Refactored gm_80160C90 name-string selection to call gm_80160980_noinline3 or fn_801609E0_noinline3 instead of spelling out the full language/alternate-name decision tree inline.
Implemented fn_80160DE8 for text/name display setup using lbLang_IsSavedLanguageUS, HSD_SisLib_803A70A0, HSD_SisLib_803A7548, lbl_803B75F8 sizing tables, and Japanese/US name lookup helpers.

Added a local struct gm_stats and implemented fn_80161C90 to accumulate match/player statistics with saturating MIN updates, including self destructs, player-standing fields, frame_count / 60 play time, winner/loser counters, and gmMainLib_8015EDBC()->x10 updates.
Implemented fn_80162170 to update persistent name-tag data via GetPersistentNameData, including vs_kos, play_time_by_fighter, and cumulative stats through fn_80161C90.
Changed gm_80162574 from raw pointer arithmetic against gmMainLib_8015EDBC() + offset 0x18 to field indexing through gmMainLib_8015EDBC()->x18[gm_80164024(arg0)].

Implemented gm_8016260C to update match-type/reset/total counters using gmMainLib_GetMatchResetCounter, gmMainLib_GetTimeMatchTotal, gmMainLib_GetStockMatchTotal, gmMainLib_GetCoinMatchTotal, gmMainLib_GetBonusMatchTotal, gmMainLib_8015ED98, gmMainLib_8015EDB0, and gmMainLib_8015EDBC.
Implemented fn_80164B48 as repeated unlock-mask checks against data at lbl_803B75F8 offsets and gmMainLib_8015ED8C bitmask state.

Implemented ranking/result helpers including fn_80165548, fn_801656A8, fn_8016588C, fn_80165AC0, and fn_80165FA4 for player/team standings, score comparisons, loser counts, winner arrays, and clamped result values.
Implemented gm_8016AC44 to search lbl_8046B668 character/costume arrays when gm_8016AE50()->x58 is non-NULL.

Updated gm_1601.h prototypes: fn_80161154 now s32 fn_80161154(MatchEnd*); fn_80165548 third arg changed to s32; fn_801656A8 returns u32 and takes u32; fn_8016588C takes lbl_8046B6A0_24C_t* and s32; fn_80165AC0/fn_80165FA4 return s32; fn_80169000 returns s32; fn_80169C54 returns void; fn_8016A4C8 returns void; gm_8016AC44 returns bool.
Refined gm/types.h layouts by replacing padding in lbl_8046B6A0_24C_t with x8, x9, and x1C fields and by exposing MatchPlayerData x38 and x3C between x35_pad and x40.
smart_moves: The PR was split out from a larger jj/the-big-gm-pr, making a large but thematically contained gm_1601 matching batch easier to land and audit.
Prototype cleanup in gm_1601.h accompanied the C implementations, preventing newly understood functions from remaining behind UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS signatures.
Struct padding was only replaced where the diff showed concrete field use, keeping type progress evidence-grounded while enabling cleaner code in gm_1601.c.

The use of small wrapper chains such as gm_80160980_noinline3 and fn_801609E0_noinline3 is a targeted matching tactic: it lets shared name-selection logic be reused while preserving call structure/codegen in callers.
Replacing raw offset arithmetic with gmMainLib_8015EDBC()->x18 indexing in gm_80162574 made the code more type-safe and decomp-readable without changing the apparent behavior.
Saturating counter updates were consistently expressed with MIN, matching common game-stat accumulation behavior and keeping overflow boundaries visible.
decomp_lessons: When a function begins to match, immediately propagate concrete signatures into the header; this PR converted several gm_1601 declarations away from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.

Do not be afraid to expose formerly padded struct fields when multiple implementations need them; MatchPlayerData x38/x3C and lbl_8046B6A0_24C_t x8/x9/x1C were added because the decompiled code referenced those offsets.

For matching old CodeWarrior output, wrapper/noinline helper chains can be preferable to pragmas or duplicating logic, especially around calls that the compiler might otherwise inline or fold differently.
Game-stat functions often need explicit saturation at u16 or u32 limits; preserving the exact clamp width matters for matching and for behavior.

A local provisional struct such as struct gm_stats can unblock matching when the global type is not yet fully named, but follow-up type consolidation should be considered once more surrounding code is understood.
Automated decomp.dev reports can reveal that a PR with many partial implementations still has high value: only two items fully matched, but 18 unmatched items improved substantially.
naming_conventions: Address-based names were retained for unknown or partially understood gm symbols, e.g. fn_80165AC0, gm_8016AC44, fn_80169C54.
Temporary matching wrappers used explicit suffixes such as _inner, _noinline, _noinline2, and _noinline3.
Unknown struct fields remained offset-like names such as x8, x9, x1C, x38, x3C, unk0, unk1A, and unk3C rather than speculative semantic names.
Known project type names were used where evidence existed: MatchEnd, MatchPlayerData, MatchTeamData, CharacterKind, GXColor, HSD_Text, HSD_JObj, and NameTagData.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced #pragma dont_inline around gm_801603B0 with inline wrapper/noinline call chains for gm_80160244 and gm_801601C4 to affect generated call structure.
Added three-level noinline wrapper chains around gm_80160980 and fn_801609E0 so gm_80160C90 and fn_80160DE8 could call a compact helper while still matching expected codegen.
Used explicit casts such as (u8) arg2, (s32), (u32), and s8/u8 conversions in ranking/stat calculations to mirror narrow-width behavior.
Used MIN with constants 0xFFFF and 0xFFFFFFFFU for saturating arithmetic in stats and counters.
Preserved loop bounds visible in the diff: player standings loops over 4 or 6 entries depending on the structure/function, team standings over 5, and gm_8016AC44 scans 27 character/costume entries.

Some code retains awkward/decompiler-shaped structure, such as repeated fn_80164B48 unlock checks and an unreachable clamp block after a break in fn_8016588C, suggesting matching was prioritized over cleanup.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported matched code for GALE01 increased to 68.85%, a +0.04% and +1624 byte improvement.
The bot reported two new full matches: gm_80166CCC (+1140 bytes) and fn_801656A8 (+484 bytes).

The bot reported 18 improvements in unmatched gm_1601 items, including fn_8016A4C8, fn_80161C90, fn_80164B48, fn_80169C54, fn_80165AC0, fn_80165FA4, fn_80169000, fn_80162170, fn_8016588C, gm_8016260C, gm_80168710, fn_80167638, gm_8016AC44, fn_80169A84, fn_80165548, fn_80160DE8, gm_80160C90, and .sdata2.
The bot noted one small regression in an unmatched item: gm_8016A22C decreased by 4 bytes, from 75.44% to 74.75%.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename struct gm_stats fields or move it into a shared header.
Search for follow-up work on gm_8016A22C after the small bot-reported regression in this PR.
Look for later cleanup of gm_80160980_noinline3 or fn_801609E0_noinline3 wrapper chains.
Search for PRs that further type lbl_8046B6A0_24C_t, MatchPlayerData x38/x3C, or gmMainLib_8015EDBC return structs.
Find later gm_1601 PRs that fully match fn_80161C90, fn_80164B48, fn_8016A4C8, gm_8016AC44, or fn_80169A84.
